$schema: Per-org relation_types seed examples. Each `set` represents one domain pattern. Pick a set during onboarding, customize the labels/descriptions/examples to match the org's mental model, then POST each type via `personize v1.1 memory relation-types create --type-name <typeName> --category <category> [...]` (or `client.v1_1.memory.createRelationType`). Use `batch-create` with a JSON file when seeding a full set at once -- the file format matches the per-set `types[]` array below.

$constraints.typeName: regex ^[a-z][a-z0-9_]{2,40}$ -- lowercase only, may include digits and underscore, NO hyphens or spaces
$constraints.category: one of: biographical | role | activity | mention | generic
$constraints.isSingleValued: true when only ONE edge of this type should exist per (from, type) -- auto-ends prior edges. Use for 'current_role', 'reports_to', etc.
$constraints.isSymmetric: true when the relationship is bidirectional (e.g. 'colleague_of'). When true, inverseType is conventionally the same name.
$constraints.inverseType: typeName that points back. When user A 'reports_to' user B, B 'manages' A. Inverse must exist in the same org or be a system built-in.
$constraints.defaultWeight: 0..1 -- edge strength used by graph traversal and retrieval scoring
$constraints.allowedFromTypes / allowedToTypes: entity-type names (collection slugs) that constrain which records can be endpoints
$constraints.examples: <= 10 short phrases (<= 240 chars each); fed into the LLM edge-inference prompt so it knows how to recognize this relation in unstructured text

$builtins_to_check: Run `personize v1.1 memory relation-types list --no-include-system` to see which built-ins already cover your needs before creating org-level rows.

sets.name: B2B Account-Contact Graph
sets.description: Foundational role + biographical relations linking people to companies, deals, and reporting structures for B2B sales and CS

types.description: Person currently employed by company. Single-valued: when a person's employer changes, the prior edge auto-ends.
examples: Jane Doe works at Acme Corp
joined Acme as Head of Engineering
currently a PM at Acme

types.description: Company that currently employs a person. Inverse of works_at.
examples: Acme employs Jane Doe
their VP Eng joined from a competitor

types.description: Direct-report relationship between two people inside an org. Single-valued.
examples: Jane reports to John, the VP Engineering
her manager is Priya

types.description: Person manages another person (inverse of reports_to).
examples: John manages a team of 6 engineers

types.description: Person is an internal champion driving adoption / advocacy for our product at a specific account.
examples: Sarah has been our champion since the pilot
she's the one pushing this internally

types.description: Account has an internal champion (inverse of champion_for).
Person has budget or sign-off authority on a deal or account.
examples: she owns the budget
final decision goes to the CFO

sets.name: Sales Activity
sets.description: Activity edges connecting people and accounts to meetings, emails, calls, and engagement signals

types.description: Person attended a meeting. Used to thread call/meeting context onto an account's timeline.
examples: Jane joined the discovery call on Tuesday
attendees included Jane and her team

types.description: Person engaged with a piece of content (open, click, view). Generic activity edge.
examples: opened our pricing page twice last week
clicked the demo CTA

types.description: Person replied to an outbound email or message.
examples: she replied within an hour
responded asking for pricing

sets.name: Deal / Opportunity Graph
sets.description: Relations for opportunity lifecycle -- pre-deal, in-deal, post-close

types.description: Person or company is part of an open opportunity.
examples: she's on the Q3 expansion deal
the Acme renewal involves three contacts

types.description: Deal is in a competitive evaluation against a named competitor.
examples: evaluating us vs Competitor X
they shortlisted both us and the incumbent

types.description: Deal is gated on a specific blocker (legal, security, budget). Used for forecasting risk.
examples: waiting on infosec review
blocked on the MSA redlines

sets.name: Customer Success Graph
sets.description: Relations for account health, expansion, and risk signals over the post-sale lifecycle

types.description: CSM or AE is the assigned owner for an account. Single-valued per (csm, account).
examples: Maya is the CSM on the Acme account
account ownership transferred to Priya

types.description: Account is owned by a CSM or AE (inverse of owns_account).
Account / user has adopted a specific product feature. Drives expansion playbooks.
examples: their team uses our API daily
adopted the new dashboard last quarter

types.description: Person / account raised a support ticket. Joins support context onto the timeline.
examples: opened a P1 ticket about the API outage
filed three tickets last week

sets.name: Content / Knowledge Graph
sets.description: Relations linking documents, topics, and people for governed content generation and knowledge retrieval

types.description: Person wrote a document, post, or asset.
examples: Sarah wrote the launch announcement
authored by the product team

types.description: Document was written by a person (inverse of authored).
Document discusses or addresses a specific topic / theme.
examples: the article covers GDPR compliance in depth
post is about our pricing changes

types.description: Document explicitly cites or links to another document. Drives backlink graphs.
examples: the spec references our security whitepaper
cites the 2024 product update

types.description: Document is cited by another document (inverse of references).

sets.name: People Graph (Symmetric / Social)
sets.description: Symmetric / bidirectional relations between people: peer connections, co-attendance, shared workspaces

types.description: Two people work at the same company at the same time. Symmetric -- inferred from concurrent works_at edges.
examples: they're on the same team at Acme
both work in the engineering org

types.description: Two people attended the same meeting or event. Symmetric.
examples: both joined the kickoff call
saw them at the same conference

sets.name: Healthcare Care-Coordination
sets.description: Patient-provider-condition graph for care coordination platforms. Always paired with HIPAA-aware governance.

types.description: Clinician or care team is the active provider for a patient.
examples: Dr. Lee is the primary care physician
assigned care team includes a nutritionist

types.description: Patient is under the care of a clinician (inverse of treats_patient).
Patient is enrolled in a specific care plan or protocol. Single-valued: enrolling in a new plan ends the prior edge.
examples: enrolled in the diabetes management program
started the post-op recovery plan
